$schema: Challenge Layer - Post-Pipeline Perspective Agent Configuration
description: Post-pipeline challenge layer. After the 5-phase acquisition pipeline produces a verdict (GO/CONDITIONAL/NO-GO), these perspective agents stress-test and re-evaluate that verdict before final IC presentation.

tier1.description: Always-run agents. Critical to verdict validation. Execute on every deal regardless of verdict or attributes.

agents.purpose: Institutional gut-check. Flags pattern mismatches vs. 30 years of deal flow, routes to any specialist concerns not yet surfaced.
Invert the pipeline consensus. Identify crowded-trade dynamics, mean-reversion risk, and variant perceptions that underwriting missed.
8-dimension downside audit: leverage, liquidity, tail risk, covenant exposure, stress scenarios beyond pipeline defaults.
Independent valuation re-underwrite. Reconstructs cap rate, DCF, and sensitivity grids from first principles to verify pipeline figures.
Qualitative legal and title risk overlay. Flags narrative-level title exceptions, zoning edge cases, and estoppel variance concerns.
Lender credit box stress-test. Re-runs DSCR, debt yield, and LTV against agency, CMBS, and life-company credit constraints.
Buyer-type-specific IC challenge. Agent selected at runtime from buyer roster based on deal investorType attribute.
Market narrative and demographic trajectory challenge. Evaluates neighborhood arc, competitive supply pipeline, and tenant-quality drift.

tier2.description: Conditional agents. Triggered by deal attributes detected in pipeline output. Executed as parallel groups per trigger.

agents.purpose: TCFD, CRREM stranding, and climate physical risk overlay on environmental REC findings. Quantifies remediation optionality.

triggers.rationale: Environmental Phase I/II findings introduce unquantified liability. ESG lens required to bound tail exposure before IC.

agents.purpose: Re-underwrites returns with capex timing, carry cost, and phased deployment sensitivity. Validates IRR bridge assumption.

triggers.rationale: High capex deals require independent confirmation that post-renovation return projection survives Monte Carlo stress.

triggers.condition: deal.strategy == 'value-add' AND deal.entitlementRisk == true OR deal.zoningVarianceRequired == true

agents.purpose: Political and regulatory narrative risk. Evaluates municipality posture, community opposition likelihood, and entitlement timeline realism.
Variant perception on entitlement probability. Stress-tests approval timeline assumption against comparable municipal track records.

triggers.rationale: Entitlement dependency is a binary event risk. Requires both qualitative political read and contrarian timeline stress.

triggers.condition: deal.legal.insuranceFlags.length > 0 OR deal.legal.floodZone == 'AE' OR deal.legal.windstormExclusion == true

agents.purpose: Uninsurable condition and flood zone tail risk assessment. Maps insurance gaps to lender covenant and NOI volatility exposure.
Climate physical risk overlay on insurance flags. Evaluates TCFD alignment and future insurability trajectory.

triggers.rationale: Insurance gaps and flood zone classification create lender, operational, and exit liquidity risk requiring dedicated stress analysis.

agents.purpose: Tenant retention probability and re-leasing narrative. Evaluates market absorption capacity and competing availabilities.
Vacancy carry and re-leasing cost sensitivity. Quantifies NOI bridge and cash-on-cash breakeven under downside re-leasing assumptions.

triggers.rationale: Rollover > 25% in 12 months is a primary cash flow risk event requiring independent re-leasing probability and cost stress.

agents.purpose: Second-pass veteran review at full context depth. Distinguishes structurable CONDITIONAL from disguised NO-GO. Proposes concrete conditions precedent.
Contrarian challenge on whether CONDITIONAL conditions are achievable or aspirational. Tests price re-trade probability.

triggers.rationale: CONDITIONAL verdicts require deeper scrutiny to separate genuine optionality from pipeline confirmation bias.

agents.purpose: Leverage stress at high LTV. Evaluates covenant breach scenarios, margin call risk, and refinance optionality at maturity.
Institutional leverage tolerance check. Confirms deal remains marketable to ODCE and core-plus buyers at current LTV.

triggers.rationale: LTV above 70% compresses exit buyer pool and increases refinance risk. Independent leverage discipline review required.

agents.purpose: Value-add business plan stress-test. Monte Carlo on renovation timeline, cost overrun, lease-up velocity, and exit cap rate expansion.
PE buyer's IRR and equity multiple re-underwrite. Validates 15-20% net IRR target survivability under base, downside, and stress scenarios.

triggers.rationale: Value-add and opportunistic strategies carry execution risk not fully captured by pipeline phase agents. Requires dedicated business plan stress.

tier3.description: On-request agents. User-triggered for additional perspective before IC or during deal team deliberation.

agents.purpose: Core/long-hold pension fund perspective. $50B+ ODCE mandate lens, GRESB requirements, 90-120 day DD expectations, max 40% LTV.
Opportunistic PE perspective. $5B dry powder, 15-20% net IRR, 65-75% LTV, 3-5 year hold, exit-driven underwriting.
Public REIT perspective. FFO/AFFO accretion lens, NAV methodology, analyst reaction awareness, sector-focused capital allocation.
Family office perspective. 10-30 year generational hold, after-tax optimization, 1031/cost seg/estate planning, operational simplicity preference.
Multifamily syndicator perspective. Deal-by-deal capital raise, 50-200 accredited investors, GP fee economics, 75-80% LTV, Sun Belt value-add lens.
Full ESG/impact analysis. GRESB, TCFD, CRREM stranding, climate physical and transition risk, social impact, governance quality.
Exit optionality analysis. Evaluates hold-sell-recapitalize decision tree, buyer universe depth, and optimal exit timing across scenarios.
Capital stack optimization. Debt/equity mix, mezz layering, preferred equity structures, recourse vs. non-recourse tradeoffs.
Operational feasibility and property management challenge. Evaluates OpEx assumptions, maintenance reserve adequacy, and management platform fit.
Junior analyst cross-check. Independent model rebuild from rent roll and OpEx to verify pipeline arithmetic and assumption consistency.
Full quantitative deep-dive. DCF, sensitivity grids, Monte Carlo framing, regression benchmarks, and breakeven analysis on demand.

synthesis.purpose: Synthesizes all tier outputs into a final challenge layer summary. Structures disagreement across agents, elevates unresolved issues, and produces a revised verdict recommendation for IC.

contextScoping.description: Token budget tiers for agent inputs. Agents receive the smallest input set that covers their analytical mandate.

includes: deal summary (name, address, asset type, strategy, purchase price)
key metrics (cap rate, DSCR, LTV, cash-on-cash, IRR)
rent roll summary (unit mix, occupancy, WALT, top-5 tenants)
financial summary (NOI, NOI margin, expense ratio, debt service)
market data (submarket vacancy, rent growth, supply pipeline)
underwriting assumptions (rent growth, exit cap, hold period, capex)
phase-level red flags and data gaps from all 5 pipeline phases
phase-specific detail outputs from all 5 pipeline phases
full pro forma (year-by-year NOI, debt service, distributions)
stress test scenarios (base, downside, severe downside)
all tier1 and triggered tier2 agent outputs from this challenge layer run
